meta.title: Horizon — Ontario Couple Retirement Planner
meta.disclaimer: Estimates only — not financial, tax, or legal advice. Engine uses pinned 2026 federal + Ontario policy with documented simplifications.
howToUseThisDoc: Read meta + purpose + architecture first for orientation.
Use modules[] for file-level roles and key exports before opening source.
Use coreDataFlows[] when tracing a UI action to engine output.
Use strategySemantics + invariants when changing tax/meltdown/TFSA logic.
Use conventionsForAgents + extensionHints before editing; avoid strategy object replacement.
After changes, run npm test; prefer driving real simulate/analyzePlan/runMonteCarlo in new tests.

purpose.summary: Client-side SPA that projects a two-person Ontario household through accumulation and retirement: federal+Ontario tax, RRSP/RRIF/LIF, TFSA, CPP/OAS, strategy search (meltdown C), Monte Carlo, and rich results UI.
primaryQuestions: Can this lifestyle be funded for life on the expected path?
Where does money come from each year (person × account)?
What tax-aware withdrawal/meltdown strategy beats naive C=0?
What is the Monte Carlo success rate under market noise?
nonGoals: Full CRA form fidelity for every credit and form
Stock/ETF ticker portfolio with live market data
purpose.strategyObjective: Lexicographic: maximize funded years, then real after-tax estate (NOT pure tax minimization). See docs/tax-minimization-memo.md.
recentProductModules: onboarding tour, demo banner, branded PDF export

architecture.pattern: Layered pure-TS engine + React UI; optional Web Worker for long jobs
architecture.dataLocality: All data on-device (localStorage drafts/scenarios); no backend

layers.role: Pinned 2026 statutory constants with sources; CPI-scale to YearPolicy
Federal + Ontario tax, OAS clawback, pension-split search
Deterministic lifetime path, spending solver, top-ups, TFSA surplus, optional modules
Seeded Monte Carlo paths + flat tuneStrategy (C grid)
Product orchestration: banded C, person ceilings, L4 share, MC pins
Off-main-thread analyze / MC / spend-to-zero / compare with progress + cancel
Inputs, results, charts, tables, export, scenarios

HouseholdInput.description: Root plan input: lifestyle, strategy, optional modules, two PersonInput

PersonInput.description: One spouse: ages, salary, savings, rooms, balances, CPP/OAS/DB, returns, unreg distribution, LIF unlock

fields.oasSoftCap: Cap effective C at year OAS clawback threshold
fields.tfsaReserveYears: L3/L4 TFSA reserve in years of spend (default 2)
fields.tfsaFirstShare: L4 share of discretionary W from TFSA first (0..1)
fields.tfsaAwareMeltdown: Scale top-ups to fit TFSA room

SimulationResult.description: Full path rows, lifetimeTax, afterTaxEstate / afterTaxEstateReal, failedAnyYear, firstDeath*, housingEstate*

YearRow / PersonYear.description: Per calendar year cashflow, tax, balancesEnd, roomsEnd, withdrawals, failed flag

TuneResult.description: Flat C search: tuned, naive, bestCeilingToday, totalTaxSaving, estateRealGain, grid points

BandedTuneResult.description: Extends TuneResult with bestCeilingBands, oasSoftCap, flatCeilingToday

MonteCarloResult.description: successRate, estateReal p10/p50/p90, netWorthRealPercentiles, failures, trial stats

YearPolicy.description: CPI-scaled brackets, credits, OAS threshold, TFSA/RRSP limits for one year

SimPath.description: Per-year inflation + per-account returns for MC trials

coreDataFlows.name: Run full plan
steps: UI validates HouseholdInput (lib/validate.ts)
engineClient.analyzePlanAsync → worker op 'analyze' or main-thread fallback
analyzePlan: validate → prepareForAnalysis (clone, stripPhantomDb, solverQuality thorough)
resolve TFSA level default l4 + reserve years default 2
tuneStrategyBanded: flat C grid → age-band coordinate descent → OAS soft-cap
person ceilings = splitCeilingByRegistered(flatC, reg0, reg1); topUpPriority higherReg; tfsaAwareMeltdown true
re-simulate with person policy; if l4 then tuneTfsaShare
metrics vs naive C=0: totalTaxSaving = Δ(livingTax + estateTax), estateRealGain
UI sets det, tune, strategy fields; clears MC; saves last plan

coreDataFlows.name: Single year in simulate()
steps: CPI step; policyFor(cpi) / buildYearPolicy
RRIF/LIF conversions; room accrual
streamsAndMins: salary, CPP, OAS, DB, mins, unreg distributions
optional payroll + GIS
spending target (phases/goals × survivor frac × CPI)
optional home sale cash → unregistered
solve discretionary W for lifestyle (bisection + pension split; quality thorough|fast)
allocateDiscretionaryW per TFSA level
person-aware RRSP top-ups under C/bands/person ceilings; scaleTopUpsToTfsaRoom if tfsaAwareMeltdown
surplus → parkSurplusTfsaFirst then unregistered
growth mid-year: B1 = B0·(1+r) + netFlow·(1+r/2)
optional first-death rollover at year end

coreDataFlows.name: Market stress test
steps: prepareMonteCarloRun pins C, bands, oasSoftCap, TFSA share from analysis (or re-analyzes if stale)
householdForMonteCarlo sets solverQuality fast + strategy pins
runMonteCarlo: generateTrialPath (lognormal returns, optional AR1 inflation) → simulate each trial
success = share of trials with !failedAnyYear
UI shows p10/p50/p90 real estate + net-worth fan

coreDataFlows.name: Tax-aware vs naive
coreDataFlows.description: Naive = same household with topUpCeilingToday=0 (no deliberate meltdown bands). Metrics: living tax, estate tax, real estate, totalTaxSaving = Δ(living+estate tax).

coreDataFlows.name: Max lifestyle that funds
coreDataFlows.notes: Binary search on spendingTargetToday; by default re-grids strategy (banded C) per trial spend level

coreDataFlows.name: A/B scenarios
coreDataFlows.notes: Side-by-side analyzePlan metrics + dual net-worth series

engineCore.notes: Every constant has source + retrievedOn + indexation (cpi|frozen|wage). POLICY_BASELINE used in footer/export. Update with docs/ANNUAL-POLICY-REFRESH.md.
CPI-index brackets/limits; frozen bands stay fixed (bracket creep).
Fed+ON, surtax, OHP, OAS clawback, pension split helpers used from simulate.
Largest engine module (~1300 lines). Optional modules: survivorship, housing, GIS, payroll, portfolio sleeves, spend phases, one-time goals. Policy cache keyed by CPI.
Lognormal returns with arithmetic mean µ; σ default 0.11; ρ default 0.85; σ=0 is bit-identical to deterministic. Progress callbacks not structured-cloneable.

analysisAndStrategy.role: Product analysis entry; stripPhantomDb; person ceilings; L4 share; MC pins
Flat C then age bands ≤71/≤80/≤120; OAS soft-cap default on; lex objective funded years → estate
L1–L4 discretionary allocation; default product level l4
L4 tfsaFirstShare grid search on deterministic path
Person top-up order, person ceiling split, surplus TFSA park, scale top-ups to room
Max funded spend; re-grids strategy per trial by default
CPP 60/65/70 × OAS 65/70 grid for both spouses
Side-by-side analyzePlan metrics + dual net-worth series
UI suggestions when plan fails funding

uiHelpers.role: Household validation; throws path blocked before analyze
JSON snapshot of inputs + MC knobs
Next Jan 1 if today after Jan 1

uiComponents.role: Root UI: tabs, run actions, hero metrics, strategy compare, plan detail (~1700 lines)
Dark theme tokens; person A lime, person B magenta
Worker ops: analyze, montecarlo, spendToZero, compare, prepareAndMc

strategySemantics.objective: Lexicographic: maximize funded years, then real after-tax estate (NOT pure tax minimization)
strategySemantics.naive: topUpCeilingToday = 0 (no deliberate meltdown); same TFSA level/rules unless share is 0
strategySemantics.taxAware: analyzePlan → banded C + person ceilings + OAS soft-cap + optional L4 share + tfsaAwareMeltdown
strategySemantics.estateTax: grossFinalBalances - afterTaxEstate (simplified unsplit final-year income baseline)

bandedC.meaning: untilAge is older-spouse age; first matching band applies
bandedC.search: flat grid via tuneStrategy then coordinate descent around flat C

tfsaLevels.l1: Taxable to C then TFSA then overflow

personPolicy.personCeilingsToday: Split of flat C by registered balances (25% floor each if both material)
personPolicy.tfsaAwareMeltdown: Scale top-ups so ~65% after-tax proceeds fit TFSA room
personPolicy.surplusParking: Fill spouse with more TFSA room first, then unregistered by unreg balance share

strategySemantics.effectiveCeiling: Year $ = C×CPI, optionally min with OAS threshold if oasSoftCap; person ceilings override flat when set

simulateConventions.ageDec31: age in year t = t − birthYear
simulateConventions.unregDistributions: On OPENING balance; reinvested; raise ACB; taxable whether or not spent
simulateConventions.spendingSolverStarts: First year either spouse is retired

solverQuality.thorough: Full split search; deterministic + strategy tuner
solverQuality.fast: Seed split from last year; MC trials (~0.15% lifetime-tax noise)

monteCarloModel.returns: gross = exp(ln(1+µ) − σ²/2 + σ·z); E[gross]=1+µ; z from shared market factor M + idio
monteCarloModel.zeroVol: emits µ exactly → bit-identical to deterministic simulate

optionalModules.survivorship: First death end of year; RRSP/TFSA/unreg rollover; ~60% CPP boost; survivorSpendFrac
optionalModules.housing: valueToday, realGrowth, sellYear, includeInEstate (PR tax-free in estate)
optionalModules.gis: Rough GIS when OAS in pay and other income low
optionalModules.portfolio: equityWeight blends equity/bond returns into accounts
optionalModules.oneTimeGoals: Extra spendToday in a calendar year
optionalModules.ceilingBands: untilAge + ceilingToday; auto-searched on full plan

results.hero: Funding, median estate (MC), lifetime tax, shortfall

engineSuites: tests/mc.test.ts — seed, zero-vol identity, AR(1), perf
testing.principle: Drive real simulate/analyzePlan/runMonteCarlo; avoid re-implementing engine in tests
testing.smoke: npm run smoke — Playwright against dev server (scripts/ui-smoke.mjs)

invariants: σ=0 MC trial path is bit-identical to deterministic simulate with same inputs
Strategy merge: never replace strategy object with a single key (wipes TFSA/bands)
stripPhantomDb: db.currentAnnualEntitlementToday<=0 must clear entire db object
Strategy objective is funded years first, then estate — not pure tax min
Worker payloads must be structured-cloneable (no functions in postMessage)
Real dollars = nominal / cpiIndex; UI must badge units
Default TFSA product level is l4 after analyzePlan prepare
Person ceilings 25% floor when both spouses have material registered balances
knownSimplifications: Estate tax uses unsplit final-year incomes (slight understatement)
Pension split is fine grid, near-optimal; MC uses solverQuality fast
Credits at lowest rate; no inter-spousal unused credit transfer
Wage-indexed limits use CPI in v1
TFSA-aware meltdown uses ~35% tax haircut estimate when scaling top-ups
Not a global multi-decade DP tax optimum
conventionsForAgents: Prefer pure functions in src/lib for new analysis; keep simulate.ts for year loop only when necessary
Preserve lexicographic funded-years → estate for strategy search unless product changes
When changing strategy fields, merge strategy objects — never replace with a single key
stripPhantomDb: zero DB entitlement clears db object (hidden accrual trap)
Worker payloads must be structured-cloneable; pass onProgress only on main/worker side, not in payload
Policy numbers: update constants-2026.ts + docs/ANNUAL-POLICY-REFRESH.md together
UI dark theme tokens in global.css; person A lime, person B magenta accents
Default start year: next Jan 1 if today is after Jan 1 (defaultStartYear.ts)
After engine changes: npm test; consider tests/fuzz and engine-ui-path
Do not invent CRA rates — source them into Sourced<> constants with retrievedOn
App.tsx is large; extract new UI to components/ rather than growing App further when possible

extensionHints.addTaxFeature: Usually strategy search in lib/* + flags on HouseholdInput.strategy + simulate year loop hooks
extensionHints.addChart: components/Charts.tsx or new panel; use chartAxis.ts + chartHover.ts
extensionHints.addWorkerOp: workers/engineWorker.ts WorkerOp + engineClient EngineJobResultMap + UI caller
extensionHints.addOptionalModule: HouseholdInput field + simulate year hook + AdvancedPlanOptions UI + test in p2-modules or dedicated suite
extensionHints.policyRefresh: Follow docs/ANNUAL-POLICY-REFRESH.md checklist; update constants-2026.ts sources

fixBugInFunding: Reproduce with sampleHousehold or minimal HouseholdInput
Run simulate() with solverQuality thorough; inspect YearRow.failed and cash sources
Compare strategy C=0 vs analyzed C
Check shortfallLevers / spendToZero if lifestyle is the issue
changeMeltdownLogic: Change search or year application; keep merge of strategy
Update analyzePlan if product defaults change
Add/extend tuneBandedC.test.ts, analysis.test.ts, tfsa-policy if allocation changes
addUiPanel: Wire into App.tsx plan detail or tools section
Prefer pure lib mapper for data; keep component presentational

fileTreeSummary.src/: App, engine (simulate/tax/mc/policy/constants), lib helpers, components, pages, workers, styles
